Name: GIAA Region 2-AAA Championships
Notes: Results loaded from the MileSplit St. Andrew's team page shared by the user and cross-checked against the linked raw complete-results page. The public files exposed 42 St. Andrew's rows, including five individual region titles, a fourth-place girls team finish with 62 points, and a third-place boys team finish with 88.5 points.
RecapTitle: Five Individual Titles Lead Strong Region Finish
RecapParagraphs: St. Andrew's turned the GIAA Region 2-AAA Championships on April 22, 2026 into its strongest postseason snapshot of the spring so far, finishing fourth in the girls standings with 62 points and third in the boys standings with 88.5 at Georgia Southern Eagle Field in Statesboro. The biggest headline was the five individual region titles won by four athletes: Aubri Rutledge in the girls 200, Lane Mandel in the girls 1600, Amari Cook in the boys 100 and 200, and Josiah Bowers in the boys high jump.

Cook's sprint double gave the meet a place in school history. His 23.03 in the 200 moved within 0.15 of Andrew Smith's school record 22.88 from 2011, and his 11.44 in the 100 edged past the 11.45 mark credited to Miller Cook in 1980. He now sits third in school history and just 0.03 seconds behind Malcolm Williams-Green record from 2011.

The other champions added their own history. Josiah Bowers tied Stephen Todd's 1981 high jump school mark of 5-6 earlier in the season and his 5-8 clearance from the region meet now firmly puts him first overall in school history. Aubri Rutledge's 27.11 in the 200 gave St. Andrew's another standout sprint performance that is just 0.26 seconds off Rebecca Suh's state championship run from 2014. Lane Mandel claimed the 1600 title even though she is in 8th grade and competing in the varsity competition.

The supporting points made the team finishes possible. Janiyah Johnson placed second in the girls shot put with a personal best of 29-1.50 that puts her within sight of Mary Wilkowski's school record of 30-2. Carson Brown finished third in the girls 3200, Pat Jackson took second in the boys long jump, Nick Brown and Ian Willis both scored in distance events, and the boys relays added second in the 4x100, third in the 4x800, and fourth in the 4x400.

Name: GIAA State Championships
Notes: Opening-day prelim results loaded from the MileSplit Live feed for the April 30-May 2, 2026 GIAA State Track Field Championships - Class AA, AAA. Field events, distance finals, and later running finals will be added after completed results post.
RecapTitle: Two School Records Fall on State Meet Opening Day
RecapParagraphs: St. Andrew's opened the 2026 GIAA State Championships with a strong first day in Locust Grove, highlighted by two school records falling in the sprint prelims and another long-standing mark coming under serious pressure. The day belonged to Amari Cook and Aubri Rutledge, who each turned state prelim opportunities into entries near the top of the school record book.

Cook broke Malcolm Williams-Green's boys 100-meter school record by running 11.39 in the prelims. Williams-Green's record of 11.41 had stood since 2015, and Cook's state mark continued a spring in which he had already moved into elite historical territory with his region sprint double.

Rutledge delivered the other record-breaking performance with a 26.50 in the girls 200-meter prelims, moving past Rebecca Suh's 2014 school record of 26.85 by 0.35 seconds. She also set a personal best in the girls 100-meter prelims with a 12.86, finishing just 0.08 seconds off the school record held by Mique McArthur from 1984.

With only the opening-day prelim results loaded so far, St. Andrew's already has a state meet headline. Field events, distance finals, and later running finals remain ahead as the April 30-May 2 championship continues.
